summary:
Briefing compiled. One note: the email send was blocked because `GMAIL_USER` / `GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD` are not set in the environment — so the briefing did **not** reach Justin's inbox. I've written the full briefing to disk (`briefings/2026-06-05-morning-briefing.html`) so it isn't lost, but **someone needs to add those credentials as secrets and re-trigger the send** for the founder to actually receive it.

Here is the complete briefing content:

---

# Guided Childhood — Morning Briefing
**Friday, 5 June 2026 · Prepared by Pulse**

## Executive Summary
The single most important thing today is a rare alignment of policy and proof: draft **KCSIE 2026** now explicitly names AI-generated and self-generated intimate images and sextortion, landing alongside the NSPCC's May sextortion findings and a fresh G7 child-safety framework — giving us both a compliance hook for schools and a national PR moment, simultaneously. On the £1M ARR mission we are at **day zero: £0 ARR, £0 pipeline, 0 schools, 0 subscribers** — 0.0% complete — so the binding constraint is not product, it's the absence of any qualified B2B conversation. The one action to take right now: **send 10 personalised outreach messages to named MAT/school safeguarding leads today**, positioning a free "KCSIE 2026 readiness" mapping sheet as the door-opener for a 20-minute paid-pilot call this week. Note ⚠️ four specialists (Scout, Navigator, Sage) failed today on a 529 overload error, so intelligence, outreach logistics, and content are stale — treat their lanes as gaps to re-run, not as "all clear."

> ⚡ **DO THIS NOW:** Personally send 10 outreach messages to named DSL/safeguarding leads, leading with the free KCSIE 2026 readiness mapping sheet, asking for a 20-minute paid-pilot discovery call this week.

---

### ⚠️ FAILED · Scout — Intelligence
Could not complete today. Error 529 (overloaded). No fresh market/competitor intelligence this morning — re-run when capacity returns.

### ✅ Guardian — Safeguarding
Draft **KCSIE 2026** modernises its language to cover "self-generated intimate images and/or videos, including those generated using AI" and reframes child-on-child abuse as a harmful-sexual-behaviour continuum — arriving exactly as the NSPCC (15 May 2026) reports a sharp rise in children being financially sextorted. DSLs must now evidence, for the standalone Ofsted safeguarding judgement (live since 10 Nov 2025), that online-safety education *specifically* addresses AI imagery and sextortion — which most legacy PSHE/RSHE materials don't. Guided Childhood's play: ship a short, age-appropriate module ("it's not your fault, tell a trusted adult, the image can be removed") that signposts Report Remove, mapped lesson-by-lesson to KCSIE 2026 wording and the Ofsted evaluation area. Tone: calm and protective, not fear-based.
Sources: [KCSIE 2026 draft changes](https://www.vwv.co.uk/insights/articles/draft-kcsie-2026-the-proposed-changes/) · [NSPCC sextortion rise (Guardian, 15 May 2026)](https://www.theguardian.com/society/2026/may/15/nspcc-sharp-rise-children-blackmailed-over-sexual-images-uk)
▸ **Action today:** Draft a one-page "KCSIE 2026 readiness" mapping sheet, usable as a free compliance aid in school outreach.
? **Open question:** Has DfE confirmed the final KCSIE 2026 publication/in-force date (expected Sept 2026)? Determines "get ahead of the consultation" vs "comply now."

### ⚠️ FAILED · Navigator — Sales
Could not complete today. Error 529 (overloaded). No pipeline/outreach status — the founder-led outreach action above stands in for it today. Re-run for target-list and sequencing support.

### ⚠️ FAILED · Sage — Content
Could not complete today. Error 529 (overloaded). No content output — the sextortion/AI-imagery module is the priority content brief to queue once Sage is back.

### ✅ Herald — PR
Freshest peg: the **G7 joint framework to protect children online** (announced 1 June 2026; welcomed by UNICEF), the first to name **AI chatbots** as a distinct child-safety risk. Coverage so far is policy-only — nobody has translated it into practical parent guidance. Target: *The Guardian* internet-safety desk. Hook: bridge global policy to the kitchen table — "the three questions every parent should ask their child about chatbot use this weekend, and why age verification alone won't catch this risk."
▸ **Action today:** Email the AI-chatbot/G7 pitch to the Guardian internet-safety desk today.
? **Open question:** Which specific Guardian journalist and contact should we use — and has the founder been quoted by them before?

### ✅ Compass — Revenue
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Current ARR | £0 |
| Target ARR | £1,000,000 |
| Gap to £1M | £1,000,000 |
| % Complete | 0.0% |
| Schools signed | 0 |
| Parent subscribers | 0 |
| Pipeline value | £0 |

**The honest read:** Day zero — pre-revenue, not early-stage. The empty pipeline is the most urgent problem: no leading indicator, nothing to forecast. The highest-leverage move is manufacturing the first signal that a school will pay; one signed school anchors pricing and produces a reference logo.
**The ONE action:** Book and run 3 discovery calls with named MAT/school safeguarding leads this week, pitching a paid pilot at a concrete annual licence price.
▸ **Action today:** Send 10 personalised outreach messages to named DSL leads.
? **Open question:** What is the target annual licence price per school? Needed to calculate schools-to-£1M and size the pipeline.

---
*Two questions block decisions and deserve one-line answers: (1) KCSIE 2026 final in-force date, (2) target annual licence price per school. Re-run Scout, Navigator, and Sage when capacity is restored.*

Guardian.output:
I have a clear, convergent picture. The two strongest signals — the NSPCC's May 2026 sextortion findings and the draft KCSIE 2026's new explicit reference to AI-generated/self-generated intimate images — point to the same single safeguarding development. Here is my read.

---

**KEY SAFEGUARDING DEVELOPMENT**

The draft **Keeping Children Safe in Education (KCSIE) 2026** explicitly modernises its language to cover **"self-generated intimate images and/or videos, including those generated using AI"** and reorders the child-on-child abuse section to track harmful sexual behaviour as a continuum — landing at exactly the moment the NSPCC (May 2026) reports a sharp rise in children being financially "sextorted" over sexual images, with Childline counselling children terrified of leaks and long-term reputational harm.
- KCSIE 2026 draft changes: https://www.vwv.co.uk/insights/articles/draft-kcsie-2026-the-proposed-changes/
- NSPCC / Guardian, sextortion rise (15 May 2026): https://www.theguardian.com/society/2026/may/15/nspcc-sharp-rise-children-blackmailed-over-sexual-images-uk

This matters for Guided Childhood because DSLs must now demonstrate, for the new standalone Ofsted safeguarding judgement (live since 10 Nov 2025), that their online-safety education *specifically* addresses AI-generated imagery and sextortion — not generic "stranger danger." Most existing PSHE/RSHE materials predate this and don't.

**How Guided Childhood should respond**

Position Guided Childhood as the bridge between the new KCSIE 2026 language and what children actually need to hear: ship a short, age-appropriate module on self-generated and AI-generated intimate images and sextortion that names the "it's not your fault, tell a trusted adult, the image can be removed" message and signposts Report Remove. Map each lesson explicitly to the KCSIE 2026 child-on-child / harmful-sexual-behaviour wording and the standalone Ofsted safeguarding evaluation area, so a DSL can drop it straight into their evidence trail. Keep the tone calm and protective — empowerment, not fear — because frightened children disclose less, not more.

YOUR ACTION TODAY: Draft a one-page "KCSIE 2026 readiness" mapping sheet showing how each Guided Childhood online-safety lesson covers AI/self-generated imagery and sextortion, with the Report Remove signpost, so Navigator can attach it to DSL and Headteacher outreach as a free compliance aid.

YOUR QUESTION: Has the DfE confirmed the final KCSIE 2026 publication and in-force date (expected September 2026), so we know whether to position our outreach as "get ahead of the consultation" or "comply now"?

Herald.output:
The G7 framework (June 1, 2026) is the freshest, biggest news peg — and its emphasis on AI chatbot risks is a sharp, underexploited angle. Since the dominant media conversation (the Guardian "tsunami of harm" piece) is about the under-16s social media consultation, the complementary angle for Guided Childhood is the AI chatbot dimension of the G7 framework.

---

**Scout finding used:** The G7 nations' first-ever joint framework to protect children online (announced 1 June 2026, agreed in Paris 29 May), which UNICEF welcomed and which centres on digital literacy, age assurance, and — crucially — addressing risks to children from AI chatbots.

**Top PR opportunity:** *The Guardian* technology/internet-safety desk — the team behind both the 26 May "A tsunami of harm" consultation piece and the February reporting on Starmer extending online safety rules to AI chatbots after the Grok scandal. The hook: the G7 framework is the first to name AI chatbots as a child-safety risk, but coverage so far has focused on policy/diplomacy — no one has translated it into what parents should actually do this week. Guided Childhood's founder can be the expert who bridges global policy and the kitchen table.

**3-sentence pitch:**
The G7's new framework (1 June) is the first time the world's largest economies have agreed AI chatbots pose a distinct safety risk to children — yet for the millions of UK parents whose kids already treat chatbots as confidants, "international principles" mean nothing without practical guidance. Guided Childhood's founder can give your readers the next-day, real-world story the policy coverage is missing: the three questions every parent should ask their child about chatbot use this weekend, and why age verification alone won't catch this risk. Available for comment today or a quick byline before the consultation news cycle moves on.

YOUR ACTION TODAY: Email this pitch to the Guardian internet-safety desk (the reporters behind the 26 May consultation and 15 Feb Grok/chatbot stories) today, leading with the AI-chatbot angle on the G7 framework.
YOUR QUESTION: Which specific Guardian journalist's name and contact should we use — and has our founder already been quoted by them, so I can reference an existing relationship?

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Current ARR** | £0 |
| **Target ARR** | £1,000,000 |
| **Gap to £1M** | £1,000,000 |
| **% Complete** | 0.0% |
| **Schools signed** | 0 |
| **Parent subscribers** | 0 |
| **Pipeline value** | £0 |

## The honest read

This is day zero. Not "early stage" — **pre-revenue**. There is no ARR, no logos, no pipeline, and no parent base. Every number on the board is a goose egg. The £1M target is 100% in front of you, and right now there is nothing in motion that converts to cash next week. An empty pipeline is the most urgent problem of all: with £0 pipeline you have no leading indicator, which means there is literally nothing to forecast or close. Pipeline is the first thing that must move — revenue is downstream of it.

The good news: at £0, the highest-leverage thing is not optimising a funnel — it's manufacturing the very first signal that a school will pay. One signed school anchors pricing, produces a reference logo, and turns an abstract mission into a real conversion rate you can actually do maths on.

## The ONE action

**Personally book and run 3 discovery calls with named MAT/school safeguarding leads this week, and pitch a paid pilot at a concrete annual licence price.**

Rationale: At £0 ARR with £0 pipeline, nothing matters except generating your first qualified B2B conversations — they create pipeline, validate price, and produce the reference logo that makes every subsequent sale easier.

---

YOUR ACTION TODAY: Send 10 personalised outreach messages to named safeguarding/DSL leads at target schools and MATs, asking for a 20-minute paid-pilot discovery call this week.

YOUR QUESTION: What is the target annual licence price per school (and per-pupil basis, if any), so I can tell you exactly how many schools = £1M and size the pipeline you need?
